message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-03_11-15-54_game-of-life-prose_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Activate test: "should keep a living cell alive with two live neighbors (survival)"
Current state: 3 tests passing
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-03_11-15-54_game-of-life-prose_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

Note: A living cell with exactly 2 neighbors should survive. Example: a "blinker" - three cells in a row at (0,0), (1,0), (2,0). The middle cell (1,0) has 2 neighbors and survives. The end cells have 1 neighbor and die. New cells become alive at (1,-1) and (1,1) which had 3 neighbors. But for a focused 2-neighbor survival test, choose simpler: cells at (0,0), (0,1), (0,2). The center cell (0,1) has 2 neighbors so survives. So next gen should at least include (0,1). 

Actually the simplest test: input cells at (0,0), (1,0), (2,0). The middle (1,0) survives - it has 2 neighbors. Test should assert that the next generation contains "1,0".

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.

content.text: I'll run the Red phase for this test. Let me start by reading the current state of the test and implementation files.

Now I'll activate the test by converting the `it.todo` to an executable test.
